clinical_criteria: LDL-C ≥190 mg/dL for primary hypercholesterolemia cases
LDL-C ≥155 mg/dL for primary hypercholesterolemia controls
30% reduction in LDL-C expected as therapy effect for patients on lipid lowering treatment
Triglycerides levels >220 mg/dL need to be flagged
Total FH score >8 points = definite FH diagnosis
Total FH score 6-8 points = probable FH diagnosis
Total FH score 3-5 points = possible FH diagnosis
Total FH score 0-2 points = unlikely FH diagnosis
FH case status assigned if total score ≥6 points
FH control status assigned if total score ≤2 points
≥2 codes separated by at least 5 days before age-specific cut-off for ASCVD
Premature ASCVD defined by gender-specific age cut-offs
Adjusted LDL-C = index date LDL-C / 0.7 for patients on lipid lowering treatment
algorithm_summary: Two-stage EHR-based phenotyping algorithm for Familial Hypercholesterolemia using modified Dutch Lipid Clinic Network (DLCN) criteria. Stage I identifies primary hypercholesterolemia cases/controls using structured laboratory data, medication history, and exclusion criteria for secondary causes. Stage II identifies FH cases/controls by scoring four diagnostic groups: (1) family history of hypercholesterolemia or premature ASCVD, (2) personal history of premature ASCVD, (3) physical examination findings (xanthomas, corneal arcus), and (4) LDL-C levels. Uses both structured data processing and natural language processing of clinical notes. Excludes patients with secondary causes of hypercholesterolemia including pregnancy and other medical conditions. Algorithm validated with sensitivity 98.3%, specificity 100%, PPV 100%, NPV 97.7%.
